prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(initialize_connection ferry_leg_1)
(initialize_connection rail_or_bus_leg_1)
(initialize_connection transfer_case_a)
(initialize_connection transfer_case_b)
(propose_local_transport_option ferry_leg_1 local_option_shuttle_1)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_connection ferry_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(mark_connection_ready ferry_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(detect_arrival_window_for_leg ferry_leg_1 arrival_window_dock_a schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(assign_local_mode_to_arrival_leg ferry_leg_1 arrival_window_dock_a local_mode_shuttle_token)
(finalize_arrival_local_mode_selection ferry_leg_1 arrival_window_dock_a schedule_slot_ferry_1 local_mode_shuttle_token)
(release_schedule_slot_reservation ferry_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(propose_local_transport_option rail_or_bus_leg_1 local_option_feeder_bus_1)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_connection rail_or_bus_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(mark_connection_ready rail_or_bus_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(detect_departure_window_for_leg rail_or_bus_leg_1 departure_window_station_a schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(assign_local_mode_to_departure_leg rail_or_bus_leg_1 departure_window_station_a local_mode_shuttle_token)
(finalize_departure_local_mode_selection rail_or_bus_leg_1 departure_window_station_a schedule_slot_ferry_1 local_mode_shuttle_token)
(release_schedule_slot_reservation rail_or_bus_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(select_and_reserve_vehicle_candidate ferry_leg_1 rail_or_bus_leg_1 arrival_window_dock_a departure_window_station_a vehicle_asset_1)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_connection ferry_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(prepare_vehicle_for_platform vehicle_asset_1 ferry_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(confirm_ferry_leg_for_manifest_binding ferry_leg_1 vehicle_asset_1)
(confirm_rail_or_bus_leg_for_manifest_binding rail_or_bus_leg_1 vehicle_asset_1)
(propose_local_transport_option transfer_case_a local_option_on_demand_taxi_1)
(release_schedule_slot_reservation ferry_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_connection transfer_case_a schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(mark_connection_ready transfer_case_a schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(release_schedule_slot_reservation transfer_case_a schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(allocate_platform_to_transfer_case transfer_case_a platform_dock_1 vehicle_asset_1)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_connection transfer_case_a schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(confirm_facility_readiness transfer_case_a platform_dock_1 vehicle_asset_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(release_schedule_slot_reservation transfer_case_a schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(assign_local_provider_to_connection transfer_case_a local_provider_shuttle_co)
(assign_equipment_attribute_to_transfer_case transfer_case_a equipment_ramp_access)
(assign_crew_to_transfer_case transfer_case_a crew_assignment_alpha)
(start_preboarding_check transfer_case_a equipment_ramp_access local_provider_shuttle_co platform_dock_1 vehicle_asset_1)
(perform_crew_facility_check_no_vehicle_confirmation transfer_case_a crew_assignment_alpha platform_dock_1 vehicle_asset_1)
(confirm_transfer_case_for_manifest_binding transfer_case_a)
(remove_equipment_attribute_from_transfer_case transfer_case_a equipment_ramp_access)
(remove_crew_from_transfer_case transfer_case_a crew_assignment_alpha)
(release_local_provider_from_connection transfer_case_a local_provider_shuttle_co)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_connection ferry_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(bind_manifest_to_connection ferry_leg_1 manifest_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(assign_manifest_and_local_option_to_ferry_leg ferry_leg_1 local_option_shuttle_1 manifest_1)
(release_schedule_slot_reservation ferry_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(propose_local_transport_option transfer_case_b local_option_shuttle_1)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_connection transfer_case_b schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(mark_connection_ready transfer_case_b schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(release_schedule_slot_reservation transfer_case_b schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(allocate_platform_to_transfer_case transfer_case_b platform_rail_1 vehicle_asset_1)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_connection transfer_case_b schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(confirm_facility_readiness transfer_case_b platform_rail_1 vehicle_asset_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(release_schedule_slot_reservation transfer_case_b schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(assign_local_provider_to_connection transfer_case_b local_provider_shuttle_co)
(assign_equipment_attribute_to_transfer_case transfer_case_b equipment_ramp_access)
(assign_crew_to_transfer_case transfer_case_b crew_assignment_alpha)
(start_preboarding_check transfer_case_b equipment_ramp_access local_provider_shuttle_co platform_rail_1 vehicle_asset_1)
(perform_crew_facility_check_no_vehicle_confirmation transfer_case_b crew_assignment_alpha platform_rail_1 vehicle_asset_1)
(confirm_transfer_case_for_manifest_binding transfer_case_b)
(remove_equipment_attribute_from_transfer_case transfer_case_b equipment_ramp_access)
(remove_crew_from_transfer_case transfer_case_b crew_assignment_alpha)
(release_local_provider_from_connection transfer_case_b local_provider_shuttle_co)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_connection rail_or_bus_leg_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(bind_manifest_to_connection rail_or_bus_leg_1 manifest_1 schedule_slot_ferry_1)
(assign_manifest_and_local_option_to_rail_or_bus_leg rail_or_bus_leg_1 local_option_feeder_bus_1 manifest_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves planning for ferry, train/bus connections, and transfer cases in a harbor station scenario. There are two transfer cases, a and b, both involving ferry_leg_1 and rail_or_bus_leg_1, sharing some resources like vehicle_asset_1.


## Examining Transfer Cases  
- The scenario involves planning for ferry, train, and bus connections at a harbor station.  
- There are two transfer cases, labeled a and b, each including ferry_leg_1 and rail_or_bus_leg_1.  

## Assessing Shared Resources  
- Both transfer cases share resources, such as vehicle_asset_1.  
- This overlap may impact scheduling and resource allocation for ferry and train/bus connections.

## Examining Transfer Cases  
- Four connection entities need initialization: ferry_leg_1, rail_or_bus_leg_1, transfer_case_a, and transfer_case_b.  
- Each transfer case requires a local transport option from three available choices: shuttle_1, feeder_bus_1, or on_demand_taxi_1, provided by local_provider_shuttle_co.  

## Assessing Shared Resources  
- Both transfer cases share vehicle_asset_1, with only one schedule slot (schedule_slot_ferry_1) available for reservation.  
- Reservations for slots and providers can be released, but no explicit action exists to release vehicle reservations once made.  

## Managing Reservations  
- Vehicle reservations are made for pairs of ferry_leg and rail_or_bus_leg, associating arrival and departure, with preconditions like legs being staged and vehicle available.  
- Once reserved, the vehicle remains assigned and unavailable, potentially limiting simultaneous use for multiple transfer cases.  

## Confirming Connections  
- Legs require actions like assign_manifest_and_local_option_to_ferry_leg, needing manifest_attached and local_option assigned.  
- Confirmation involves steps like confirm_ferry_leg_for_manifest_binding, requiring staged legs, buffered status, and vehicle reservation.

## Managing Reservations  
- To reserve a schedule slot, it must be available, but it's currently reserved by ferry_leg_1, potentially needing release first.  
- Releasing the slot (e.g., from ferry_leg_1) makes it available again, but actions like finalize_arrival_local_mode still require a reserved slot, creating a dependency.  

## Staging Connections  
- Staging a leg involves detecting windows, which requires the connection to be ready and the slot reserved.  
- After detection, staging can proceed via local_mode (e.g., shuttle_token) without a provider, but finalizing still needs a reserved slot, complicating the process.  

## Exploring Local Mode Options  
- The local_mode path allows staging without a provider, using tokens like shuttle_token, but requires a reserved slot for finalization.  
- Releasing the slot after staging isn't possible without impacting finalization, as it depends on the slot remaining reserved.

## Exploring Transfer Case Paths  
- Transfer cases can follow alternative paths, like facility readiness and preboarding checks, leading to transfer_ready_for_finalization.  
- Confirming transfer cases for manifest binding requires ready_for_finalization, not passenger_registered, and sets confirmed, ready_for_manifest.  

## Managing Preboarding Checks  
- Completing preboarding checks needs legs buffered and possibly a reserved slot on the transfer case, setting final_checks_passed.  
- Finalizing and confirming transfer cases requires ready_for_finalization, final_checks_passed, and sets confirmed, ready_for_manifest.  

## Handling Facility Readiness  
- Confirming facility readiness needs a ready transfer, allocated platform, assigned vehicle, and reserved slot, setting facility_ready.  
- Acknowledging priority and confirming facility requires arrival_confirmed vehicle, priority_applied, and assigned status.  

## Allocating Platform Resources  
- Allocating a platform to a transfer case requires a ready transfer, candidate vehicle, available platform, and reserved vehicle, setting allocated.  
- Preparing a vehicle for platform use ties to staged ferry legs, requiring reserved vehicle and connection_reserved_slot, even for rail departures.  

## Addressing Vehicle Constraints  
- Vehicle reservations are global per vehicle, tied to specific ferry and rail legs, with no explicit release action.  
- Once reserved, vehicles remain unavailable, potentially blocking simultaneous use for multiple transfer cases sharing vehicle_asset_1.

## Examining Slot Reservation Conflicts  
- Finalizing both ferry and rail legs requires a reserved slot, but only one slot can be reserved at a time, creating a conflict.  
- Staging one leg and releasing its slot before staging the other is necessary, but vehicle reservation needs both legs staged and buffered simultaneously.  

## Sequencing Leg Preparation  
- To reserve a vehicle, both ferry and rail legs must be staged and buffered, even if the slot is currently reserved for one leg.  
- Preparing the vehicle requires the slot to be reserved specifically for the ferry leg, necessitating slot release and re-reservation.  

## Resolving Vehicle Preparation  
- After reserving the vehicle, release the slot from the rail leg to make it available, then reserve it for the ferry leg.  
- This allows preparing the vehicle for the platform, ensuring connection_reserved_slot conditions are met for the ferry leg.

## Exploring Resource Constraints  
- Many actions, like confirm_facility and apply_constraint, temporarily require a reserved slot for the transfer, but it can be released and re-reserved as needed.  
- All paths to confirm a transfer case seem to need this slot temporarily, especially for facility readiness and preboarding, before releasing for later leg binding.

## Assessing Shared Resource Usage  
- Shared resources like equipment, crew, and providers (only one available each) must be assigned to a transfer case for certain paths, such as preboarding and constraint checks.  
- Actions like finalize_after_crew and confirm_transfer_case don't release these assignments, but actions like remove_equipment_from_transfer_case can free them post-confirmation, though not required until then.

## Investigating Resource Release  
- Remove actions exist for equipment, crew, and provider (e.g., remove_equipment_from_transfer_case, release_local_provider_from_connection), but aren't triggered by confirmation steps.  
- For priority and passenger groups, no remove actions are shown, suggesting sequential processing or unused paths may avoid conflicts with limited availability.

## Analyzing Connection Readiness  
- All paths for transfer cases and legs require connection_ready, which starts with proposing a transport option.  
- Proposing an option assigns it to a specific connection, making it unavailable for others, and there are only 3 options but 4 needs.  

## Investigating Option Constraints  
- No action exists to unassign or release a local transport option except through confirmation, creating a bottleneck.  
- Sharing options between transfer cases or legs isn't possible due to assignment restrictions, requiring sequential processing.  

## Examining Confirmation Paths  
- Confirming a transfer case, such as via finalize_transfer_case_after_crew_verification, requires connection_ready, looping back to the option issue.  
- Actions like apply_operational_constraint_profile_to_transfer_case also depend on connection_ready, reinforcing the need for available options.

## Processing Sequential Steps  
- Legs can propose and confirm transport options (e.g., option1, option2), releasing them afterward, allowing reuse for transfers.  
- This means only 2 options are needed effectively, as legs free options after confirmation, enabling sequential processing.  

## Managing Shared Manifests  
- Legs bind and release the single manifest during their process, freeing it for subsequent legs without conflict.  
- Transfer cases don’t bind manifests, avoiding usage conflicts and simplifying resource allocation.  

## Handling Shared Resources  
- The single vehicle can be reserved for both legs, and platforms (e.g., platform_dock_1 for transfer_a, platform_rail_1 for transfer_b) are assigned separately.  
- Equipment, crew, and provider (one each) are assigned to a transfer, used, then released after confirmation for reuse, ensuring sequential flow.  

## Exploring Constraint Paths  
- Transfer_case_a can use the constraint path (e.g., wheelchair access available), but transfer_case_b cannot (e.g., bike_storage not available initially).  
- This suggests using alternative paths, like preboarding, for cases where constraints fail, ensuring flexibility.  

## Preparing Facility Readiness  
- Facility readiness for transfers requires a temporary reserved slot, connection_ready, and platform allocation (e.g., via allocate_platform).  
- Preboarding checks then need assigned equipment, provider, and facility readiness, ensuring all shared resources are sequenced properly.

## Confirming Transfer Case A  
- Transfer case A can reserve a slot and confirm facility readiness with platform_dock_1 and vehicle_1, meeting all prerequisites like allocated platform and reserved slot.  
- After facility readiness, assign equipment (e.g., ramp access), provider, and crew to transfer case A, then pass preboarding and crew checks, leading to confirmation.  

## Releasing Resources  
- After confirmation, release equipment, crew, and provider from transfer case A, making them available for reuse.  
- The platform and local option remain assigned to transfer case A, but this doesn't block further processing.  

## Handling Transfer Case B  
- Transfer case B faces an issue with no available options, as all three options are currently in use (shuttle, feeder, taxi).  
- To proceed, confirm a leg (e.g., ferry_leg_1) first to free an option, requiring reserving a slot and binding the manifest.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
